lesson: Use Path.with_suffix to swap a file extension in one call instead of string-slicing the path; it operates on the final suffix only, so '.log.bak' replaces just '.log'.
Rely on the 'with open(...) as f' block to guarantee f.close() runs on any exit path; check f.closed after the block instead of manually tracking close calls.
task: context-managed file handles close on exit

lesson: Iterate 'for line in f' to stream a huge text file line-by-line instead of f.read() or f.readlines(), since the loop never materializes the whole file in memory.
task: line-streaming a huge file vs loading it whole

lesson: Use tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) when another handle must reopen the file by name (required on Windows), then os.unlink it yourself; use TemporaryDirectory for scratch dirs that self-clean on context exit.
Make writes atomic by creating the temp file with tempfile.mkstemp(dir=same_directory_as_target), fsync its fd, then os.replace(tmp, target) so readers never observe a partially written file.
task: atomic config write via tempfile + os.replace

lesson: Open a large fixed-width-record file with mmap.mmap(fileno, 0, access=ACCESS_READ) and slice by byte offset to random-access records; the OS pages in only the touched bytes instead of loading the whole file.
task: mmap random access into a large fixed-width-record file

lesson: Run binary search directly over an mmap'd sorted fixed-width record file using struct.unpack_from at computed byte offsets, giving O(log n) disk touches with zero full-file load for on-disk index lookups.
task: mmap-backed binary search over sorted on-disk records

lesson: To grow a memory-mapped file, first f.truncate(new_size) on the underlying file object, then remap with a fresh mmap.mmap call; also call mm.flush() explicitly since ACCESS_WRITE mmap edits aren't durable until flushed.
task: in-place mmap mutation with file growth via truncate+remap
